questions.question: In synchronous digital circuit design, what is the 'setup time' requirement for a flip-flop?
options: The minimum time the input data must be stable AFTER the active clock edge.
The maximum time the input data can change BEFORE the active clock edge.
The minimum time the input data must be stable BEFORE the active clock edge.
The total propagation delay from the data input to the output.
The time required for the clock signal to transition from low to high.

questions.question: Which type of memory is typically used for CPU caches due to its high speed, but is more expensive and less dense than other volatile memory types?
options: Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM)
Static Random Access Memory (SRAM)
Read-Only Memory (ROM)
Flash Memory
Magnetic Core Memory

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, what is the primary function of a logic synthesis tool?
options: To convert a gate-level netlist into a physical layout (mask data).
To simulate the behavioral description of the RTL design using testbenches.
To translate an RTL (Register Transfer Level) description into an optimized gate-level netlist using a technology library.
To verify the physical layout against the schematic (Layout Versus Schematic).
To assign specific locations for each standard cell on the silicon die (placement).

questions.question: When designing a System-on-Chip (SoC) with multiple clock domains, which technique is commonly used to safely transfer single-bit control signals between two asynchronous clock domains without violating setup or hold times, or incurring metastability issues?
options: Using a global asynchronous reset signal.
Inserting a single synchronizer flip-flop.
Implementing a simple combinational buffer.
Employing a two-flop synchronizer (or 'double flip-flop synchronizer').
Bypassing the clock signal through a phase-locked loop (PLL).

questions.question: What is the primary architectural principle behind the design of a Reduced Instruction Set Computer (RISC) processor, contrasting it with Complex Instruction Set Computer (CISC) architectures?
options: To minimize the number of general-purpose registers available to the programmer.
To execute complex instructions that can perform multiple memory-to-register operations in a single cycle.
To provide a very large number of addressing modes for memory access.
To use a simpler, fixed-length instruction format for easier decoding and pipelining.
To primarily rely on microcode for all instruction execution.
